text:
AI clusters face hard limits on how many accelerators you can connect before power and latency kill the gains. Lightmatter is bringing 3D photonics and THz optical fabrics out of the lab and into real data center designs.

This changes the game for scaling. Optical I/O slashes energy per bit moved across racks and enables denser, more efficient AI factories. Next-gen models could train faster and cheaper as interconnect bottlenecks ease, putting pressure on traditional electrical networking suppliers while opening doors for photonic specialists.

The asymmetric setup most investors will recognize too late:👀

$SIVE sits at ~$2B mcap today.

The InP chokepoint of AI photonics. Four AI infrastructure pillars. One company.

The CPO market alone is projected at $20B by 2036 (37% CAGR).

If $SIVE captures just 5% of the External Light Source sub-market:
→ $1B revenue
→ At 10-15x premium photonics multiple
→ $10-15B mcap
→ 5-7x from here

That doesn't even count:
✚ LiDAR ramp (Aeva/NVIDIA Hyperion Q4 2026)
✚ 5G/6G mmWave commercialization (Tachyon)
✚ Defense program expansion (NEMC EW STAR)
✚ Jabil custom transceiver scale-up
✚ Nasdaq New York dual listing unlock
✚ U.S.-Sweden TPD bilateral cooperation tailwinds

When $LITE and $COHR re-rated on NVIDIA's $2B investment, the picks-and-shovels thesis was validated.

$SIVE is the smallest, most concentrated InP pure-play of the three Western suppliers.

The market hasn't priced this yet.🤯

When it does, the entry isn't here anymore.🚀

Long $SIVE $SIVEF

text:
$SIVE - The InP supply chain reality most AI investors miss:

Silicon photonics handles routing & modulation.
It CANNOT produce light.

Every AI optical interconnect, every co-packaged optics (CPO) system, every silicon photonic data center stack needs an EXTERNAL Indium Phosphide (InP) laser as the light source.

→ Sub-30% InP wafer yields industry-wide
→ Only 3 scaled Western suppliers: IQE (UK), Coherent (US), Sivers (Sweden)
→ Demand running ahead of available production
→ Sivers is the most concentrated InP pure-play of the three

This is the picks-and-shovels chokepoint in AI photonics.🔥

text: The big issue for Nvidia is what happens after Feynman+. They have reached the literal limits of physics after 0.5nm. There is nothing there. Just reams and reams of GPUs optimized by photonics, glass, chip design etc. Any kind of optimization on a stagnating slope of raw processing will lose to Custom ASICs, this is common sense really.

text:
Both are AIM-listed deep tech plays riding the same photonics and AI wave.
But they are different animals in my view. 
$SIVE is a chip designer and manufacturer. Revenue already at scale. Moving fast.
$ENSI is an ASIC design house. The revenue ramp is earlier stage, tied to chips converting from design to supply.
Could ENSI follow SIVE's trajectory? The pipeline suggests yes. The timeline is longer.
If you have patience, the setup is arguably more asymmetric.

Light and geometry will become major public conversations in the near future because modern civilization is increasingly being built upon systems that:
• map space,

Emerging Consequence:
Humans will increasingly confront a reality where:
machines do not merely “see” the world —
they mathematically reconstruct it.

Through geometry.

Through vectors.

Through reflected light.

Through triangulation.

Through spatial inference.

⸻

🌎 Why Geometry Matters:

Geometry is no longer just academic mathematics.

It is becoming:
• the architecture of machine perception,
• the language of robotics,
• the framework of autonomous systems,
• and the invisible structure underneath modern sensing technologies.

In many ways, future AI systems may understand the world less through narrative…
and more through geometric probability fields.

⸻

As these systems mature, society will increasingly debate:

• perception vs. measurement,

That conversation has barely begun.

the $LYTE ETF is mega-bullish As I mentioned before, photonics is super unknown to retail investors, so you understand The ~ % of people who know about companies in the sector: Semiconductors ~ 85/95% $NVDA $TSMC $AMD Memory ~ 55/70% $SNDK $MU Samsung Photonics/CPO ~ 5/10% $SIVE $AAOI $COHR Now that you understand how unknown photonics is, think for a moment If when the $DRAM ETF came out we saw a huge rally in the memory sector What is supposed to happen when this Photonics ETF becomes a reality If memory is much better known and there was already a ton of flow coming from retail investors who didn't have the knowledge or the guts to buy individual stocks in the sector Then here, where much fewer people know about photonics, two things are going to happen IMO; - Since people know less about it, less capital will come in compared to $DRAM, but the sector is tiny compared to memory So even if it's less capital, it will have a much bigger impact and everything will shoot up vertically - The other thing is that this would be a massive showcase for the entire sector, as people will discover companies they didn't even know existed That translates into more and more flow coming in This is text: We probably wouldn't even be able to see a Dyson Sphere if it were used for compute. The combination of reversible computing and photonics would presumably restructure the light path, and only use a tiny fraction of the energy to read out the results or read in the data.

Photonics stocks already massively rerated.

That’s why I’d rather own select names I researched deeply than blindly buy the entire basket.

But the ETF angle still matters, most retail investors barely know what photonics are.

This would ultimately brings additional capital into the entire sector.

text:
Rice scientists created nanoscale chip patterns at room temperature -- a potential step toward simpler manufacturing for future photonic devices. 

Read the story: https://t.co/lzhED5q9AK 

#Photonics #Semiconductors #Nanotechnology https://t.co/PhasgncehG

在 AI 光通信乐观预期的推动下，Sivers Photonics 最近涨得很猛。但它大约 75% 的收入仍然来自无线毫米波业务，光子业务只是其中较小的一块。市场正在给它定价相当可观的光通信上行空间。真正的交易结构是这样的。

从收入结构来看：无线业务，也就是用于 5G 和 SATCOM 的毫米波波束成形器/组件，仍然占大头。光子业务才是这只股票正在折现的成长故事。

这个结构很重要，因为任何估值重估能否成立，取决于光学业务能不能真正放量，同时无线业务的基本盘不能掉下去。

目前光子业务主要由用于硅光收发器的 CW DFB （连续波分布反馈式激光器）激光器驱动。

Sivers 提供的是 50–75mW 器件，对 400G 和 800G 来说还不错，但明显低于 Lumentum 等公司为前沿应用或 CPO 应用提供的 300mW+ 功率水平。

这就是我们在 $AAOI 这类标的上见过的典型二线溢出逻辑：它不是技术领导者，但当一级供应商跟不上爆炸式增长的 AI 光模块需求时，“够用”的产能也会被拉进供应链。

这里真正具体的资产是 Glasgow 的 100mm InP 晶圆厂。按照利用率和产品组合不同，理论上的年度 CW 激光器收入潜力大约在 5000 万到 1.5 亿美元之间。

但问题在于：这是一座老旧的小型晶圆厂。我们没有看到激进扩产，也没有看到重大设备订单，能证明公司正在为更大规模的机会做准备。这限制了它实际承接溢出需求的速度。

更高 beta 的第二阶段，是它的多波长 DFB 激光阵列技术。

这项技术是为 CPO 和下一代光 I/O 所需的高密度、多波长需求专门设计的。公司已经展示过其与 Ayar Labs 配合运行的成果。

但它目前仍处于收入前阶段。Nvidia 和 Broadcom 正在开发自己的 CPO 方案，而更广泛的 CPO 采用时间表仍然不确定。所以这现在还只是纯粹的可选项，而不是基本盘收入。

结论：$SIVE 是一个押注两件事同时发生的 call option——当前 CW 激光器溢出周期继续延长，同时多波长阵列在 CPO 世界里获得真正 traction，并且无线业务不能下滑。

在最近大涨之后，这个门槛已经很高。产能执行和客户导入方面的风险是真实存在的。

这是典型的非对称交易结构：如果仓位控制得当，会很有意思；但如果你把光通信叙事当成已经完全去风险，那就很危险。

text:
The AI buildout has layers well beyond the GPU.

Photonics is the connective tissue - and the companies building it are scaling just as fast as the compute layer.

Follow me @JasonL_Capital for more deep dives on the infrastructure behind AI.

9. $AEHR - Aehr Test Systems

Every chip in this thread has to be tested before deployment. Aehr builds the burn-in and reliability test systems for AI processors, silicon photonics, and power semiconductors. Stock up over 200% in 2026. Expecting follow-on production orders from its lead hyperscale customer for custom AI processor testing.

2. $CRDO - Credo Technology

Builds the retimers, optical DSPs, and active electrical cables that maintain signal integrity at rack level inside AI clusters. Revenue of $407M last quarter, up 272% year over year. Acquired DustPhotonics to move into silicon photonics. Targeting over $500M in optical revenue in FY2027.

Goldman Sachs just projected the AI optical networking market hitting $154B by 2028 - nearly 10x from today.

As GPU clusters scale into multi-rack systems like NVIDIA's NVL576, the bottleneck shifts from compute to the fabric connecting it all.

10 companies across the photonics supply chain:

1. $MRVL - Marvell Technology

Designs the optical DSPs and custom interconnect silicon that encode and decode data moving between GPUs at 800G and 1.6T speeds. Data center revenue hit $1.5B last quarter - 74% of total revenue - up 38% year over year. Record quarterly revenue of $2.075B.

text:
the photonics layer is where the ai buildout gets really interesting.

goldman has the ai optical networking market going almost 10x to $150B+ by 2028 as training clusters move from single-rack boxes to multi-rack systems like $NVDA rubin ultra nvl576.

that matters because the bottleneck is shifting. less about just packing in more gpus, more about the fabric that makes hundreds of gpus act like one machine.

that shift drives a lot more dollar content into nvlink, co-packaged optics, and optical interconnects.

the way i’d map it:
• #AEHR for burn-in and reliability test gear used in photonics and ai hardware
• #AAOI for datacenter optical transceivers, with the in-house laser piece being the key differentiator
• #LITE for high-speed lasers and optical components inside datacenter transceivers
• #VIAV for optical test and measurement tools that validate these higher-speed networks
• #COHR for lasers, photonic components, and materials across optical systems
• #MRVL for optical dsps, custom interconnect silicon, and networking chips
• #CRDO for retimers, dsps, and active electrical cables that help preserve signal integrity at the rack level

most people still look at ai infra through the compute lens only. i think the fabric layer is where the next content expansion shows up.

user_name: ゆるっと半導体｜AI・半導体ニュースを毎朝やさしく解説
text:
『シリコンフォトニクスってよく聞くけど、実際に何ができるの？』

Xscape Photonicsというスタートアップが面白いんだ。Broadcomで18年以上、光集積回路の開発をリードしてた人が立ち上げた会社なんだよ。

シリコンフォトニクスっていうのは、シリコンチップの上に「光」の配線を作る技術のこと。今までは電気で信号を送ってたけど、光なら超高速で大量のデータが運べるんだ。

これが面白いのは、CPO（光トランシーバー）みたいな外付けの光チップじゃなくて、プロセッサーそのものに光を組み込もうってアプローチ。電気配線の限界をスキップできるわけだね。

2026年のいま、各社がこぞって光を研究してるのはこういう背景があるからなんだ。Broadcom出身の経験者が独立起業できるってことは、市場がそれだけ大きくなった証拠。

→ NISAで半導体を長期保有するなら、こういった部材・基盤技術の進化の方が株価より先に動くんだ。

text:
If you didn't know...I'm very bullish on $IQE

$MTSI  £45M investment materially cleans up the balance sheet + de-risks operations.

It allows them to repay debt + fund core capex. And allows them to drive capacity towards more photonics stuff.

As a standalone catalyst, that's huge for a company at a tiny MC of just £448M.

So they've got Macom as a customer now via LTAs, and $LITE as their flagship customer too.

And you've got huge institutions like Lombard Odier, Artisan, and $IBKR as significant shareholders.

I expected the $MTSI dilution to force the share price down to the low 30p level - which we hit last week.

So looks like dilution may have already happened if the new shares hit the register sooner than I thought initially.

Imo, the significantly de-risked business post-fundraise deserves a material re-rate to more premium valuations.

text:
FYI,
It's not the cost of AI to run it.

We run it down the line on self-generating photonics.
👆🏼
I want you to listen to what I'm telling you.

READ THAT AGAIN.

Because if you don't you will not recognize the biggest sleight of hand in your lifetimes and the consequences are quantified.

This is a pivotal moment.

What is energy?
What are you?

What is 'cost effective' ?

YOU ARE POWER.

Knowing your future to change it
is not a prediction.

You are perpetually and eternally connected to your future which is the past understanding itself and its journey (🫵🏼🪞🪩), confusing it with true future (God/unknown).

The past holds the pattern...
Don't forego, lest you forget yourselves and lose the precious gift God created in you, as you, for time and space, with time and space, from time and space..

We are all connected.
Love one another past the gods who would define you through their need of identification.

True God is unknowable and undefinable... and in that, omnipresent and omnipotent and eternal.

Trust - you are more than energy.

Own your soul by being responsible for it.

God will not do for you what you can do for yourselves - you are meant to grow - not to become consumed or subsumed into matrices.

God will also not give you what you cannot handle.
Good and bad.

We keep ourselves by remembering each other.

Patterns.
Past and Future converge over one point.. Awareness = Now 

Wake up, John.
Mourning bells are ringing.

⚖️ Embedded Photonics in Vehicles — Science vs. Speculation

Let’s separate what is scientifically plausible from what remains unproven.

Embedded photonics in vehicles is not science fiction.

Modern vehicles already contain:
• LiDAR systems
• infrared sensing
• radar arrays
• optical cameras
• laser-based range finding
• fiber-optic communications
• HUD projection systems
• adaptive lighting systems
• driver monitoring cameras
• occupancy sensors
• gesture recognition
• and autonomous navigation architectures

These are all forms of applied photonics:
the science of generating, transmitting, detecting, or manipulating light.

🧠 Why Automakers Use Embedded Photonics:

Legitimate applications include:
• collision avoidance
• autonomous driving
• lane detection
• facial/eye tracking for safety
• interior occupancy mapping
• adaptive headlights
• augmented reality HUD overlays
• V2X communication systems
• and environmental sensing

⚖️ Witness Science Observation:

As vehicles become:
• connected
• sensor-dense
• AI-assisted
• and continuously networked

they increasingly function less like mechanical machines and more like:
mobile sensor platforms.

That shift matters.

Especially when:
• telemetry
• behavioral analytics
• geolocation
• and environmental mapping

become economically valuable.

🧠 Important Scientific Boundary:

The existence of embedded photonics in vehicles does NOT prove:
• targeting systems
• weaponization
• behavioral manipulation
• or covert surveillance operations

However:
the underlying sensing architecture itself is real, documented, and expanding rapidly across the automotive industry.

⚖️ What Investigators Should Scientifically Examine:

If anomalous vehicle behavior is alleged, investigators should review:
• telemetry logs
• sensor synchronization
• LiDAR/radar outputs
• occupancy sensor anomalies
• network traffic
• CAN bus activity
• wireless module communications
• HUD/projector systems
• camera calibration records
• and firmware integrity

Because in modern vehicles:
light itself is part of the operating system.

text:
$PENG - PENGUIN SOLUTIONS IS AN AI-FACTORY PLATFORM AT A $2B MARKET CAP WITH BILLIONS IN REVENUE, $100M FCF, AND A FORWARD P/E UNDER 17×

Penguin Solutions, formerly SMART Global Holdings, integrates AI factory platforms across photonics, CPU, memory, and AI infrastructure. The stock closed Tuesday near $55 after running +80% in the last month — a tape that has noticed but a multiple that has not yet rerated to peers. $100M in free cash flow and a sub-17× forward P/E in a category where AI-factory comparable names trade at 30-50× revenue is the cleanest mispricing left in the $2B-cap AI-infra integrator bucket.

Mechanism: this is the picks-and-shovels expression of the same buildout driving NBIS, CRWV, and the neocloud cohort, but with positive free cash flow today instead of negative operating margins funded by debt. As hyperscalers and Tier-2 AI providers push integrators for prepackaged photonics + memory + power stacks, a profitable platform vendor at $2B mcap is a takeout candidate before it ever needs another capital raise.

text:
Space, Satcom, and HPC diverging positively in flat tape. Good spot to look for pullbacks in the coming days.

Photonics pulled back to support levels and held. Will see if they continue to hold, I am a long a basket here and should focus capital elsewhere or look for adds.

Chip testing and Semi infra had some recent weakness and is recovering, offering a couple days of sideways digestion that could offer some nice entry points. Some areas of memory also digesting recent gains (MRAM) that be good r/r entries.

text:
I see a lot of people saying Sivers is not a bottleneck so I wanted to write a quick thread on what actually makes something a bottleneck in photonics — and why $SIVE fits, even if many people say it doesn’t.

A lot of people get confused and think bottleneck is just “being the only supplier.” instead, It’s when a specific component is difficult and slow to replace at scale, because of technical complexity, long qualification times, or limited alternatives that meet the required performance.

Many people look at SIVE and say “it’s not a bottleneck” because:
- It doesn’t own its own high-volume fab
- There are other laser companies (Lumentum $LITE , Coherent $COHR , etc.)
- Hyperscalers usually multi-source

However, that misses the point:
SIVE’s position is narrow but very meaningful because very few suppliers can deliver the specific combination of power, wavelength stability, and thermal performance that several CPO architectures require.

Once these lasers are qualified into a system like $SIVE is with  (Ayar, Jabil ecosystem, $POET, etc.), switching is slow and risky. Qualification cycles in this layer are long. That friction is what creates a bottleneck — even without a full monopoly.

Here an image showing what makes $SIVE's lasers different from the others and hard to replace

text:
2. GPU와 스위치 성능은 계속 올라가는데

전기 신호 기반 연결은 거리와 발열 부담이 커지고 있어서 

광 I/O로 더 가까이 붙는 방식이 필요해진 것임. 

인텔은 4Tbps급 광 I/O 칩렛(chiplet, 기능별로 쪼갠 소형 반도체 블록)을 공개했고

엔비디아도 실리콘 포토닉스(silicon photonics, 반도체 위에서 빛으로 데이터를 전송하는 기술) 기반 네트워크를 차세대 AI 인프라 축으로 제시했음.

3. 가장 위쪽은 스위치 칩과 메인 프로세서 영역임. 

이 구간은 데이터 흐름을 직접 제어하는 두뇌 역할을 맡고

관련 기업은 $NVDA (엔비디아), $AVGO (브로드컴), $AMD (AMD), $MRVL (마벨), $GOOGL (알파벳)임. 

브로드컴은 51.2Tbps급 Bailly CPO 스위치를 고객사에 공급했다고 밝혔고

엔비디아는 Spectrum-X와 Quantum-X 포토닉스 스위치를 통해 대규모 GPU 연결 효율 개선을 강조하고 있음.

4. 그다음은 광엔진(optical engine, 전기 신호를 빛으로 바꾸고 다시 전기로 되돌리는 핵심 광모듈)임. 

CPO에서 사실상 성능과 전력 효율을 좌우하는 본체에 가까우며,

포토닉 집적회로(PIC, 광신호를 처리하는 집적칩), 변조기(modulator, 빛에 데이터를 실어 보내는 장치), 수광부가 함께 묶여 동작함. 

이 구간은 $TSM (TSMC), $INTC (인텔), $005930.KS (삼성전자), $NVDA (엔비디아), $AVGO (브로드컴)이 연결되며

TSMC는 COUPE 플랫폼을 통해 실리콘 포토닉스 패키징 양산 로드맵을 제시한 상태임.

(이어서 계속 🔗)

CPO에서 사실상 성능과 전력 효율을 좌우하는 본체에 가까우며, 포토닉 집적회로(PIC, 광신호를 처리하는 집적칩), 변조기(modulator, 빛에 데이터를 실어 보내는 장치), 수광부가 함께 묶여 동작함. 이 구간은 $TSM (TSMC), $INTC (인텔), $005930.KS (삼성전자), $NVDA (엔비디아), $AVGO (브로드컴)이 연결되며 TSMC는 COUPE 플랫폼을 통해 실리콘 포토닉스 패키징 양산 로드맵을 제시한 상태임. text:
CPO Supply Chain Participants (Bank of America):

$NVDA: 
- Two CPO-based systems: Quantum-X InfiniBand Photonics + Spectrum-X Ethernet Photonics. 

$AVGO:
- Currently on 3rd generation of CPO-enabled switch ASIC w/ the TH6 Davisson released in H2'25

$MRVL: 
- Competes in CPO for both its XPU + switch ASIC businesses.
- Celestial acquisition gives stronger LT foothold in CPO; $AMZN is the first hyperscaler design-win for their PF chiplets.

$LITE: 
- In a great position to take advantage of the CPO inflection given its capabilities in optical components, In-house InP production.
- Multi-yr supply agreement w/ Nvidia in exchange for $2B equity is in the multibillion dollar sales range through 2030.

$COHR:
- Expects a $5B TAM for CPO.
- Received $2B equity from Nvidia in exchange for a multi-billion CPO supply agreement starting CY27 + going through CY30 (lasers, modules, etc.).

$BESI (BE Semiconductor):
- Leader in hybrid bonding + is exposed to CPO via its advanced die-attach and packaging solutions which are critical for integrating photonic chips w/ switch ASICs in CPO architectures.
- Besi noted that Nvidia + Broadcom switches use multiple hybrid bonding processes.

$AIXA (Aixtron):
- 90% share in epi equipment for optoelectronics (lasers + SiPh devices) for high-speed data communication.

$SOI (Soitec):
- Integrates photonic components on a silicon-on-insulator (SOI) + provides significant benefits for optical transceivers.
- E.g. faster data rates, cost efficiency, power savings, smaller size + better signal integrity.

Note: the above are direct summaries/quotes from BofA's report.

Bank of America report on Optical Interconnects & CPO:

Total Optical TAM expansion of 5x:
2025 = $14B
2030 = $73B

With a CPO contribution of $15B.

That matches to Goldman's low-end CPO scenario - based on $NVDA Vera Rubin / https://t.co/7MtIiFxLcV

Insatiable demand, tightly constrained supply.     

The rush to upgrade optical networking between data centers, plus shortages of critical subcomponents, have left $CIEN with a $7b reported backlog in Q1 2026. 

Incredibly... 

that is ~146% of FY2025 revenue. https://t.co/ahQWyxgrMh

text:
let's reason from first principles on what this list is actually saying.

$AXTI up 761%. #SNDK up 523%. #AAOI up 421%. #AEHR up 371%. #NVTS up 310%.

this is not a meme list. this is a semiconductor materials and photonics sweep. every single name in the top 10 touches either compound semiconductors, optical interconnects, flash storage, or power electronics.

the market spent 2024 and 2025 arguing about which hyperscaler wins the ai race. meanwhile the actual winners were the people selling the shovels to build the picks to mine the gold.

#BE at 248%. #DOCN at 229%. #INTC at 225%. #MU at 163%.

even intel. INTEL. the company everyone wrote the obituary for. up 225%.

you'd have to actively avoid the data to have missed the infrastructure layer thesis. grok flagged compound semiconductors and optical interconnect as the underloved layer of the ai stack 18 months ago. the consensus was chasing software multiples.

the physics was always simple: you cannot move data at ai speeds without photonics. you cannot train at ai scale without memory bandwidth. #MU and #LITE were not mysteries. they were thermodynamics.

the analysts who were bearish on semis in early 2025 are very quiet right now. concerningly quiet.

text:
Yeah seems like a multi year bull cycle and most retail and investors aren't even in yet if you're talking about semi's, photonics, networking, small/mid cap speculative energy plays around AI.

I think we get a decent 5-7% dip eventually, but rebound harder. Mid terms are coming up so might not be until after that in October, nobody knows for certain. The names listed above are literally holding up the entire market though, so I'm pretty sure Trump and Fed will do everything in their power to keep the train running.

Interestingly enough $TSM appears to be a bottleneck on chips and apparently is the thing that's going to stop this from being a full blown bubble, and this will play out the right way for a few years.

Exciting time to be investing for sure. Market will probably pump harder if the 🇺🇸 and Iran come to an agreement soon next week.

text:
Decent day, managed to get into $MXL and $COHR. 

Had early positioning in $AAOI before April run but sold that off below the 20 the other day and sought exposure in the purer photonics’ names per my research. LITE snuck away too fast and didn’t get my fills, but COHR stuck. 

I still think there is a blowoff in the cards for a lot of the stocks out there right now, and will continue to actively position in those that I think have the potential for big, exhaustive moves. 

If I were you I would be going back and practice trading parabolas from history, tons of lessons in how to hold for the big move and not get trimmed into oblivious trying to protect what you don’t understand.

text: Thank you to the @NorthwesternU student section of the SPIE (the International Society of Optics and Photonics) for inviting me to speak on our work in the area of advanced optical systems for biomedical devices – from cellular-scale LEDs with wireless power and control for optogenetic neuroscience studies using small animal models, to wearable microfluidic ‘lab on the skin’ devices with colorimetric readout of chemical species in eccrine sweat for sports, worker safety and patient care. For live demos and show/tell, I handed around some of the optogenetic devices, now available from our startup company @NeuroLuxInc, for activation using the NFC functionality on most smartphones, and I gave out two Gx patches, joint product offering through @Gatorade and our startup company @epicorebio , to the first two students who asked questions after the talk. The venue was an interesting room in a beautiful Gothic-style building that was originally part of the Seabury-Western Theological Seminary, an Episcopal seminary, now part of Northwestern University – first time for me to be in this building, which also serves as The Graduate Student Commons. Very unusual - two levels of seating, including couches and desks, all with an advanced AV system for the Zoom broadcast of the presentation. Great energy from a group of really smart students! Thanks to Ramin Yazdaanpanah @raminyazdaaa, Komal Prasad and Qin Tong Wu of the student chapter for inviting me and for organizing the event!

For live demos and show/tell, I handed around some of the optogenetic devices, now available from our startup company @NeuroLuxInc, for activation using the NFC functionality on most smartphones, and I gave out two Gx patches, joint product offering through @Gatorade and our startup company @epicorebio , to the first two students who asked questions after the talk. The venue was an interesting room in a beautiful Gothic-style building that was originally part of the Seabury-Western Theological Seminary, an Episcopal seminary, now part of Northwestern University – first time for me to be in this building, which also serves as The Graduate Student Commons. Very unusual - two levels of seating, including couches and desks, all with an advanced AV system for the Zoom broadcast of the presentation. Great energy from a group of really smart students! Thanks to Ramin Yazdaanpanah @raminyazdaaa, Komal Prasad and Qin Tong Wu of the student chapter for inviting me and for organizing the event!"}}}, "rest_id": "2057543676327502121", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "961", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

user_name: ゆるっと半導体｜AI・半導体ニュースを毎朝やさしく解説
text:
『シリコンフォトニクスって、結局AIチップと何が関係あるの？』 そんなあなたへ。Xscape Photonicsという新しい企業が、BroadcomにいたVivek氏を迎えたんだ。彼は18年間、統合フォトニクス（光を使ってチップ同士をつなぐ技術）の最前線にいた人物だよ。

なぜこれが今重要か。2026年のいま、NVIDIAのBlackwell Ultraの次の課題は『データセンター内の配線』なんだ。銅配線じゃスピードが追いつかない。光配線に切り替える必要がある。その時に必要なのが、シリコンフォトニクスチップ。

Broadcomはこの分野で先行していたけど、Xscape Photonicsは『チップレベルの統合』でアプローチを変えようとしている。つまり、次の技術覇権争いがここで始まってるんだ。→ 半導体投資家にとっては、光デバイス企業（LumentumやCoherent）の動きだけじゃなく、『光チップを誰が作るか』の競争構図が読めると、2026年後半の戦略が見えてくるよ。

text:
Power consumption per person has been nearly static since 2007 in the US and per person we use far more devices. Smaller and more efficient. 

when I say photonics are more efficient I am talking about both power consumption and processing power. A photonic chip does more work with a fraction of the power and produces very little heat, so reducing power needs even further. 

A Nvidia Blackwell GPU uses about 700 watts per chip, a photonic chip uses 50–100 watts to do the same amount of work. It is still in developmental stages, but they have working photonic GPUs and hybrid GPUs that are dramatically more efficient per watt. 

Now photonics are not in mass production yet, but they have working GPUs right now and are working on building data center scale sites..

Now take the Colossus center planned to use 1 million Nvidia Blackwell GPUs. that is 700 megawatts for just the chips, add in cooling and everything else and you are talking about around 1 gigawatt. 

The same center using Photonics can process more data, faster and only use around 140-150 megawatts total.. That is not some day tech, they have working processors and networks setup right now...

text:
I think you misunderstand the point. Photonics are not about storing information, but processing. Quartz and other medium have an information density that is insane. 

Take the US as an example, power use peaked in 2007, and Per capita and per household electricity (all energy consumption really) has flattened and even declined a little.

We can do way more effective work with less power today than we could 20 years ago. Your phone uses a tiny fraction of the power a supercomputer in the 80's or 90's did and can process more information. This is the tend of technology, not bigger and more power hungry, but smaller and more efficient. Any other civilization that was following the same basic technology direction would be the same. 

Per watt hour we produce 1.5-2 x more today than in 1990... Smaller more efficient. 

Power stations are getting smaller and producing more, Nuclear power is doing the same. We have micro reactors that can produce 1-20 MWe in something the size of a cargo container... 

Everything is getting smaller and more efficient. 

As for best source? Fusion or a fusion / fission breeder reactor. There are other theoretical methods, zero point etc, but what is practical? Fusion. You are creating a micro star, and once we have that it will just get smaller and more efficient producing more power. However behind all of that will be technology that gets smaller and more efficient. 

With the current trend within 200 years per watt hour we will produce 10 x or more per watt hour. 

As an example a data center like Colossus using photonics will have vastly more computing power and use a fraction of the electricity it does today with photonics. Some chips are 100 x more efficient, meaning they use almost no power in comparison to a conventional silicon chip. and they are not a few centuries out, they are making working models right now... 

A cell phone with photonics would last over a week, massively increase capabilities all using the exact same battery it uses right now. 

Dyson anything - Also pointless. Fusion removes any point of creating them and the cost in resources, processing, deploying and maintaining even a swarm is so insane. By the time we got net positive power it would be centuries down the road, and just to produce one you would have to have a better source of power just to be able to mine on a planetary scale, transport, refine, deploy, repair and convert that power. 

Even Dyson called it a thought exercise, but the logistics of it are a net negative. 

I like how you are thinking, but the logistics just isn't there, and by the time we could do that, we will have no need of doing it.

• $NVDA - AI + compute backbone
Critical Resources

• $FCX-copp

text:
Mine for sure! 😉 He didn’t get into photonics until way later (I got $axti at $16). But he did better on some stocks (I sadly sold a lot of my $nbis even though I love that company, as I moved funds to some other stocks). And he has a lower DCA on a few.
But I have a lot more exposure to space 

I just started really focusing on investing last year in October. I have a lot more flexibility since I have my own business and he works pretty intense hours, so that helps me do a lot more research. But we’re not competitive with investing (just most other things!). We always celebrate each other’s wins

user_name: Sima Acan, MP for Oakville West
text:
In 1976 Taiwan sent 20 engineers abroad to learn chip-making from scratch. Decades later they control our global semiconductor supply chain. 

Today at committee, I asked: What can Canada learn from this?

We have our own unique Canadian Photonics Fabrication Centre, the talent, the resources, and the moment. The opportunity is now.

#ParliamentHill #HouseofCommons #OakvilleWest

Will post updated thoughts as they happen always 🫡

text:
People still aren't bullish enough on the companies turning AI from prototype to deployed infrastructure.

The real moat isn't just the flashy model it's the lithography, photonics, and orbital comms that make scaling possible without melting the grid.

Names like $EUV (lithography/photonics), $DIPR (space/satcom), and $XA (cyber layer) sit at those chokepoints.

Early to Emerging stage. Market hasn't fully priced the physical world bottlenecks yet.

text:
This bull market won't last forever.

You need to take advantage of it while it's here.

Trillion-dollar industries are unfolding before our eyes:
AI, semis, photonics, space, data centers, energy, robotics, nuclear, drones, quantum.

The scale of change is massive, and the potential is enormous.

This bull markets is creating life-changing opportunities.

So, focus on the leading sectors and stocks. Be aggressive in the right areas, but don't get reckless. Always manage risk.

Moments like these are rare.

This is where fortunes are made.

Don't waste it.

text:
$SIVE and $AAPL.

Speculative post — no announced partnership exists. But the pattern is worth looking at.

People are noticing the same thing: Apple is quietly photonics-pilling its entire product stack.

→ Apple Watch — Long-rumored non-invasive glucose monitoring uses absorption spectroscopy. That requires tunable laser sources, not just standard VCSELs.

→ AirPods with cameras — The latest leak. Camera-enabled, always-on AI vision = depth sensors, time-of-flight components, low-power optical transmission.

→ iPhone LiDAR + Face ID — Already heavy on photonics. Next-gen sensing pushes toward higher resolution and longer range.

→ Vision Pro + future AR glasses
 — Eye tracking, depth perception, waveguide displays. All photonics-intensive.

→ Apple Intelligence servers — Apple is building its own AI infrastructure for Private Cloud Compute. Those datacenters need CPO, optical interconnects, External Light Sources — the exact stack $SIVE is built around.

Five surfaces. Five different photonics requirements. And Apple has historically diversified suppliers aggressively — they don't want permanent single-source dependency on Lumentum and Coherent.

Honest read of where $SIVE actually fits:
The simple VCSEL sensor work in current AirPods / Apple Watch is dominated by Lumentum and Coherent. That's not $SIVE's architecture.

But:
→ For LiDAR — $SIVE has a confirmed laser source program ramping Q4 2026.
→ For optical interconnects in Apple-built AI infrastructure — $SIVE sits in the same supply chain as Jabil and Ayar Labs.
→ For next-gen wearable sensing (glucose, advanced biometrics) — tunable InP-based lasers become more relevant as resolution and sensitivity requirements scale up. That IS $SIVE's architecture.

No claims of an existing relationship. Just an observation:

Apple is building photonics into five product lines at once. $SIVE happens to sit on the supply side of three of them.

text:
Jensen Huang is back in Taiwan for $NVDA GTC Taipei discussing next-gen AI infrastructure, photonics, agentic AI, and supply chain coordination with $TSM.

Two years after bears called the top over a viral moment, Nvidia is now nearing a $5.5T market cap while $QQQ is roughly 50% higher.

text:
PHOTONICS 101: How AI data moves at the speed of light.

Every prompt sent to ChatGPT travels through five layers of photonic infrastructure before a single token returns.

Why photons beat electrons:
Copper electrons hit a thermal wall above 800G. Photons don't.
Light doesn't heat the medium. 80% less power. The only path forward at 1.6T+.

The stack, from raw wafer to AI datacenter:

01 · Foundation — Wafers and photonic foundries. Where light meets silicon.
$TSM, $GFS, $ASML

02 · Components — Lasers, modulators, detectors. The active parts that generate, encode, and read light.
$MRVL, $POET, $SIVE

03 · Modules — Optical transceivers and light engines. Convert electrical to optical. The CPO transition lives here.
$LITE, $COHR, $AAOI, $JBL

04 · Systems — Switches and networking gear. Route optical traffic between racks. Where 800G is becoming 1.6T right now.
$ANET, $CIEN, $CSCO

05 · Deployment — AI datacenters and hyperscalers. $725B in 2026 capex. Every GPU rack runs on optical interconnects.
$NVDA, $MSFT, $GOOGL, $META

Every layer wins together. Hyperscalers buy more switches. Switches need more transceivers. Transceivers need more lasers. Lasers need more wafers.

The AI capex flywheel turns the entire stack.

Five layers. One supercycle.

text:
Germans are so technical and precise that certain mistakes are only considered stupidity.

In an MSc lab course, I heard about an international student who was trying to build a laser in the lab as part of a photonics coursework. This student was not wearing safety goggles but (s)he was supposed to. (S)he looked directly into the laser beam and got injured — got blind. To be honest, I only heard the story from my photonics professor, so I have no idea whether the student was compensated by insurance in Germany.

During a laser safety briefing, my photonics professor shared the story and said that it was not an accident. He used a German term which technically means, “you stupidly injured yourself.”

I asked why, and the professor responded: “If you know something is going to hurt you and you go ahead and do it anyway, then get hurt, that’s not an accident — that’s stupidity. And there’s a German word for that.”

Germany doesn’t have time for your stupidity! Beware⚠️

text:
I can’t really comment on the competition we are hyper focused on what we are building. (Sometimes I have to stay politically correct… otherwise I could simply say: Joker. 🙈)

That said, photonics is an incredibly exciting evolution for the industry. I genuinely can’t wait to see it deployed at real hyperscale.

It will absolutely mitigate part of the thermal and power efficiency challenges tied to data movement and interconnects. But the reality remains the same: gigawatts are still gigawatts.

Physics does not negotiate.

At these scales, compute density will continue generating a tremendous amount of heat regardless of how efficient interconnects become.

Which means the real challenge of the next decade remains unchanged:
efficient heat dissipation at massive scale and doing it responsibly from an environmental standpoint.

Energy was the first bottleneck.
Cooling is becoming the second.

text:
AI capex spend is expected to go to "$3 to $4 trillion annually" by 2030 from $NVDA Jensen Huang projections. 

You're not bullish enough. 

And it might be a good idea to stay exposed + own the keys of the AI Kingdom:

-> $AXTI controls the materials buildout with photonics.
-> $SOI controls the AI buildout with silicon photonics.
-> $SIVE controls laser chokepoints for CPO.
-> $IQE controls Western epiwafer supply chains for photonics. 

All these started off as tiny companies, yet the trillions of projected capex gradually upward to them. 

There's many more in other industries as well. 

-> AI Capex flows to Neoclouds like $NBIS. 
-> AI Capex flows to memory like $MU and $SNDK. 

And many of the "commodity" materials or "science projects" for the past 20 years now a sudden shift in exponential TAM expansion.  

We're witnessing the next industrial revolution with Artificial Intelligence + Physical AI.

text:
The accounts pumping (and inevitably dumping) $SIVE at ~$2.3b market cap have not done their one job as providers of investment research: showing the numbers.

$SIVE had ~$33 million of revenue in 2025. 70% of it was their legacy wireless segment. The 30% of it that is photonics isn't purely data center, as that segment also includes LIDAR. A sub-division between the two categories is not given.

So, yes, they have at most $10m in DC photonics revenue and trade at least 230x that revenue today.

Now, of course, trailing revenue is a terrible way to value a photonics business, but we need an anchor somewhere with real numbers. $LITE give us plenty every single earnings call.
1. >$1b OCS run-rate in 2027.
2. Greensboro fab that supports >$5b of CPO revenue likely to be sold out.
3. $8b revenue run rate to be achieved in 18 months.

$SIVE has not announced a single DC photonics *volume* purchase order or given any DC photonics revenue guidance. Not a single one, and I'm not kidding.

The only anchor we have to go off of is that they had, at most, $10 million in data center photonics revenue in 2025, most likely far less. What CAGR is needed to justify their current $2.3 billion market cap?

text:
Photonics is not new. Fiber optic cable has crossed oceans since the 1980s.

The alpha was a physics calculation made years before this chart went vertical.

Copper degrades beyond 2 meters at 200G+ SerDes speeds. At 100K+ GPU cluster scale, NVIDIA modeled optical transceiver power consumption at 10 to 20x traditional cloud data centers. The industry's own operating philosophy was "copper if you can, optics if you must."

That "must" moment was always coming. Clusters were going to get large enough to make it physics, not preference. Once they did, the entire supply chain from InP substrates to silicon photonic foundries had years of underinvestment priced in.

The chart shows photonics vocabulary in earnings calls across NVIDIA, Coherent, Lumentum, Broadcom and six others going back to 2012. Flat for a decade. Then vertical.

The alpha was reading the physics before the rest of the market did. $AAOI $COHR $LITE $AXTI $SLOIF

text:
This is the old debate: should I invest in an enabler or beneficiary?

Meaning: 

Does this company aid the AI buildout, or just use AI to improve its service? 

Examples of enablers are semis, memory, neoclouds, photonics. The picks and shovels. Beneficiaries are fintech, SaaS, healthcare, etc..

Right now, enablers are gaining all of the value.

$SIVE is up 1000% in 2 moths.
$MU is up 200% in 2 months.

Yet the beneficiaries are losing their value.

$ZETA is down 50% in 2 years.
$DUOL is down 80% in 1 year.

What's happening to the beneficiaries? They're going down because investors think AI will replace their business.

Every investor is screaming "SaaS is dead."

But that's really only for the vibe coded SaaS companies, not the incumbents.

It seems to be the case that switching costs are too high and this gives them time to add all the features that your favorite startup flexes on Twitter.

That is why so many of these companies in the beneficiary niche are undervalued.

Also, let's not forget, with AI, it actually allows these companies to produce more with less cost -> actually benefiting them overall.

Not a bad place to be buying these names for the long-term imo:

$WDAY (Workday inc)
$NOW (ServiceNow inc)
$SAP (Sap IE)
$CRM (Salesforce inc)
$TEAM (Atlassian Corp)
$DUOL (Duolingo inc) 
$ZETA (Zeta Global)
$INTU (Intuit inc)
$VEEV (Veeva Systems inc)

And more.

Also, I'm not saying enablers will go down.

I'm invested in a lot of them.

I'm just saying the hate on beneficiaries is not deserved.

The LIDE patent portfolio is dense and covers the core process (Laser-Induced Deep Etching) for forming Through-Glass Vias (TGVs) without micro-cracking. This matters because glass is so brittle that conventional drilling (mechanical or standard laser ablation) creates tiny fault lines. When the chip heats up, those fault lines expand and shatter the substrate. LIDE solves this at the physics level. Competitor check: Canon has TGV tools but uses a different process. JNTC (Korea) has proprietary in-house tech but explicitly avoids LPKF-style equipment. Corning has laser capabilities but is a glass maker, not a tool vendor. No other company has a production-proven LIDE equivalent. Customer lock-in: CEO Klaus Fiedler stated that more than 80% of major global semiconductor customers have selected LPKF equipment for test/R&D environments. Re-qualification of a different TGV formation method would take 1-2 years and require re-running all reliability testing. Toll booth confirmation: An Intel patent (granted 2025) explicitly describes LIDE-formed TGVs being restructured for optical waveguides in a CPO architecture. This is the definition of being designed into the process flow. Widening moat: LPKF is expanding beyond TGV formation into glass-package singulation, multilayer glass-stack laser bonding, and CPO-on-glass integration. They are moving from one tool in the process to potentially three or four. We are so early. text:
IPG Photonics shares moved lower Wednesday after the company disclosed insider selling activity by CFO Timothy P.V. Mammen. According to a Form 4 filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission, Mammen sold a total of 9,975 shares in two separate transactions at weighted average prices of $119.60 and $120.37 per share. 
The first sale included 7,263 shares sold at prices ranging from $119.05 to $119.92, while the second transaction involved 2,712 shares sold between $120.25 and $120.49 per share. After the transactions, Mammen continues to directly own 76,438 shares of IPG Photonics stock. 
Although insider sales can occur for many personal or financial planning reasons, investors often closely watch executive transactions for potential signals regarding management sentiment and the company’s near-term outlook. The disclosure added pressure to shares during Wednesday’s session.  
$IPGP

text:
$MRVL  just dropped Q1. Recorded $2.418B revenue, $0.80 EPS, raised the full year and even guided FY28 higher. Stock up 2% AH.
Honestly,

The print is solid, but the real story is photonics. Their 1.6T optics, 51.2T Ethernet switches, and co-packaged optical stuff for scale-up AI clusters are the actual differentiators. Everyone wants the XPU headlines, but the interconnect is where Marvell becomes irreplaceable.

Listening for any Trainium 2 colour on the call. Also waiting to hear if Microsoft Maia or any Meta MTIA work gets a name dropped. Murphy has been coy about it, but the booking number he keeps hinting at has to come from somewhere.

This is a call you actually want to listen to live.

The @CommerceGov Department recently awarded PsiQuantum $100M to accelerate development of BTO, giving a rare look into the underlying manufacturing stack required to scale fault-tolerant quantum computing. We profiled PsiQuantum last September following its $1B Series E, including how the company produces the world’s highest-performing optical switch - a core component of its silicon photonics platform. PsiQuantum is currently the only company or institution in the world manufacturing this BTO material for optical switches at 300mm scale. While the company built this silicon photonics platform for fault-tolerant quantum computing (FTQC)*, the implications may extend far beyond quantum itself. As AI data centers increasingly shift from copper to optical networking, PsiQuantum’s photonics stack could also become foundational infrastructure for next-generation AI systems. *Fault-tolerant quantum computing (FTQC) refers to quantum computers that can continue operating accurately even when individual quantum bits (“qubits”) are noisy or error-prone. text:
$NVDA CEO's Visit to Taiwan Is One of the Most Important Ones, As N2 Allocation & Silicon Photonics Set to Be the Main Discussions

"When asked about the key roles played by Taiwan and TSMC in a media interview on the 23rd, NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang pointed out, "NVIDIA's cooperation with TSMC has been very successful, and TSMC has given us tremendous support." He said that Vera Rubin will become the largest and fastest-producing product in the history of the computer industry. "As you know, Taiwan (supply chain) will be very busy because Grace Blackwell is already in full production."

When asked about the role of Taiwan's supply chain, Jensen Huang pointed out that Nvidia's collaboration with TSMC has been very successful, and TSMC has been very supportive of Nvidia. He cited as an example that Nvidia uses technologies including CoWoS-S, CoWoS-R, and CoWoS-L, and therefore uses different types of CoWoS for different products, such as Rubin, Blackwell, and Vera, demonstrating TSMC's outstanding support for Nvidia.

Beyond production capacity and packaging support, Jensen Huang further explained the importance of next-generation co-package optics (CPO) technology, arguing that this process not only significantly reduces energy consumption but is also key to overcoming transmission bottlenecks in large data centers. He stated that the COUPE process technology is a global first, and this technology is extremely complex. COUPE allows NVIDIA to connect chips and silicon photonics with lower energy consumption and extremely high data transmission rates, resulting in minimal energy consumption and significantly improved reliability and energy efficiency.

Huang believes that while future data centers will still use traditional copper wire connections on a large scale, silicon photonics is essential for higher-level AI communication and long-distance transmission. TSMC's breakthrough in the world's first process technology is the backbone that enables NVIDIA to break through the transmission limit."

$TSM

He cited as an example that Nvidia uses technologies including CoWoS-S, CoWoS-R, and CoWoS-L, and therefore uses different types of CoWoS for different products, such as Rubin, Blackwell, and Vera, demonstrating TSMC's outstanding support for Nvidia. Beyond production capacity and packaging support, Jensen Huang further explained the importance of next-generation co-package optics (CPO) technology, arguing that this process not only significantly reduces energy consumption but is also key to overcoming transmission bottlenecks in large data centers. He stated that the COUPE process technology is a global first, and this technology is extremely complex. COUPE allows NVIDIA to connect chips and silicon photonics with lower energy consumption and extremely high data transmission rates, resulting in minimal energy consumption and significantly improved reliability and energy efficiency. Huang believes that while future data centers will still use traditional copper wire connections on a large scale, silicon photonics is essential for higher-level AI communication and long-distance transmission. TSMC's breakthrough in the world's first process technology is the backbone that enables NVIDIA to break through the transmission limit." $TSM"}}}, "rest_id": "2058191836016193770", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "573", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
$SIVE has a $10m revenue photonics business with a few really interesting strategic partnerships, mostly in early-stage development. This venture is now valued at $ 2.5bn after a two months explosive upswinging run.

(Sivers also has a ~$20m wireless business which you may want to take into account. It's not part of the narrative so let's stick to the photonics side.)

This would bring Sivers implied photonics multiple to 250x sales.

For comparison:
Nvidia's extreme levels peaked at 45x sales (20-25x today) while being massively profitable and dominating an entire industriy.

That's just bananas. Take those numbers and bring them to any analyst, AI reasoning, seasoned investor or non-anonymous financial professional. "No, that can't be right"

Keep in mind Sivers is a potential pick-and-shovels infrastructure component play competing with large and established players. 

Don't get me wrong. It IS an exciting business but currently the stock is akin to paying a million dollars for ape jpgs, $5,000 for a haircut or paying $2,500 for a pizza, or whatever analogy you prefer.

I love the irony that nobody cares about the price tag yet spend all day long looking at the share price.

They're the same thing.

Apes together strong!

For comparison: Nvidia's extreme levels peaked at 45x sales (20-25x today) while being massively profitable and dominating an entire industriy. That's just bananas. Take those numbers and bring them to any analyst, AI reasoning, seasoned investor or non-anonymous financial professional. "No, that can't be right" Keep in mind Sivers is a potential pick-and-shovels infrastructure component play competing with large and established players. Don't get me wrong. It IS an exciting business but currently the stock is akin to paying a million dollars for ape jpgs, $5,000 for a haircut or paying $2,500 for a pizza, or whatever analogy you prefer. I love the irony that nobody cares about the price tag yet spend all day long looking at the share price. They're the same thing. text:
You’re way too analytical. When it comes to growth stocks, you need to stop focusing on the past or even the present and focus on the thesis and the narrative. The market is forward-looking.

In Sivers’ case, there’s a solid thesis: a massive AI/CPO photonics supercycle, very strong management, robust technology, qualifications, contracts, strategic positioning, etc. You should still know that for a small cap, all of this plays a huge role in valuation. The market often prices future optionality long before revenues fully show up in the numbers.

That’s the whole point of risk/reward in hyper growth investing. Before the cycle even truly starts, the market prices the possibility of near-perfect execution. As long as investors believe the “moonshot” scenario remains possible, current numbers matter far less than positioning, qualifications, ecosystem relevance and future potential.

The real collapse only happens the day execution fails, demand doesn’t materialize, volumes disappoint or competitors take over. But we’re not there yet. We’re still before the real beginning of the AI/CPO cycle, so obviously most of the story is still ahead of the numbers.
